items.summary: Fix the grave number change logic in the backend so that updating a grave's plot number never deletes or detaches the parent alley. The operation must be atomic: copy all grave data (including all deceased persons, owner, status, payment info) to the new key, then remove the old key, while keeping the alley record fully intact and all its grave references updated correctly.

Ensure alleys are always stored and retrieved in a consistent, predictable sort order. Alleys must be sorted alphabetically by name (or numerically if names are numeric) in all backend query responses. This sort must be applied to every function that returns alleys or cemetery layout data.

Apply consistent alley sorting on the frontend in all components that render alleys (AdminGraveTileMap, CemeteryLayoutManager, GraveTileMap, GraveResultsList, and any other alley lists). Sort alleys alphabetically/numerically on the client side as a safety fallback, so even if backend order changes the UI always shows a predictable order.

Add a safeguard in GraveEditDialog (and any other edit dialogs) that warns the admin before saving a plot number change, clearly stating that this operation will change the grave identifier. Show a confirmation step specifically for plot number changes distinct from the general save action, to prevent accidental data-loss operations.
